newsSince: No material new company-specific news since June 9. Keli has completed development of six-axis force/torque sensor product series for humanoid wrists/ankles and industrial/collaborative arm ends and supplies torque/6D sensors to Siasun-type customers, but these are continuations of an existing commercialization arc rather than a fresh catalyst.

sources.label: Keli 603662 six-axis force/torque sensor product series (moomoo)

verdicts.rankRationale: Force-sensing angle the humanoid crowd underweights remains intact; flat price (-0.2% YTD) keeps under-ownership/rerating room vs over-extended peers. No fresh catalyst since June 9, so the prior up-case has largely played out; nudge up only, not a re-rate.
verdicts.bull: Differentiated 6D force/torque exposure, completed product series, deep under-ownership, no YTD froth to unwind.
verdicts.neutral: Real stack fit but commercialization is incremental; alpha already partly captured at score 76.7.
verdicts.bear: China component competition intensifies; force-sensing content per robot stays small and ASP-pressured.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Design-win/volume orders into a named humanoid program
verdicts.primaryRisk: Commoditization of force sensors and China price competition
